stories.title: Settings Menu Reorganization
stories.description:
Reorganize the Settings modal from 11 tabs to 7, improving scannability with section labels and subtabs. No settings are removed or renamed — this is purely a structural/UI change.

## Tab Changes
- Themes + Priorities → **Appearance** tab with subtabs (Theme | Priority Display)
- Updates + What's New + MCP + Help → **About** tab with subtabs (Updates | What's New | Integrations | Help)
- Notifications absorbed into **General** as a section
- **General** and **Timer** get section label dividers for visual grouping

## New Tab Structure (7 tabs)
1. General (section labels: Task Behavior, Quick Add, My Day, Notifications, System, Keyboard Shortcuts)
2. Projects (unchanged — existing General/Statuses sub-navigation)
3. Appearance (subtabs: Theme | Priority Display)
4. Labels (unchanged)
5. Timer (section labels: Presets, Breaks, Repetition, Behavior, Alerts)
6. About (subtabs: Updates | What's New | Integrations | Help)
— Logout button at bottom of sidebar (unchanged)

## Subtab UI Pattern
Use existing connected button group pattern from settings toggles:
- Container: `flex rounded-lg border border-border overflow-hidden`
- Active: `bg-accent/12 text-accent px-3 py-1.5 text-[11px] font-bold uppercase tracking-widest`
- Inactive: `text-muted hover:bg-foreground/6 px-3 py-1.5 text-[11px] font-bold uppercase tracking-widest`
- State managed via `useState` within each subtabbed component
- Content switches via conditional rendering

## Section Label Pattern
Use existing app-wide pattern: `text-[10px] font-bold uppercase tracking-[0.3em] text-muted` with `mt-6` spacing above (except first section in a tab).

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Extract inlined components from UnifiedSettingsModal.tsx (1067 lines → ~100)
- Create `GeneralSettingsContent.tsx` — extract GeneralSettings, LaunchAtLoginSetting, AutoArchiveSetting. Add 6 section labels. Absorb NotificationsSettingsContent inline as NOTIFICATIONS section (enable toggle, conditional lead time dropdown, conditional info box).
- Create `ProjectsSettingsContent.tsx` — extract ProjectsTab, SortableProjectRow, ProjectColorPicker, ProjectDeleteSection.
- Create `WhatsNewSettingsContent.tsx` — extract WhatsNewContent, WhatsNewDot, parseChangelog, useChangelog.
- Update UnifiedSettingsModal.tsx to import from new files.

### Step 2: Create AppearanceSettingsContent.tsx
- Subtab bar (Theme | Priority Display), default: Theme
- Renders ThemeSettingsContent or PrioritySettingsContent based on active subtab
- forwardRef the theme handle through to ThemeSettingsContent so parent modal can intercept dirty state
- When switching from Theme subtab to Priority Display subtab with unsaved theme changes, trigger the existing unsaved-changes warning toast (same behavior as switching top-level tabs)

### Step 3: Create AboutSettingsContent.tsx
- Subtab bar (Updates | What's New | Integrations | Help), default: Updates
- Renders UpdateSettingsContent, WhatsNewSettingsContent, McpSettingsContent, or HelpSettingsContent based on active subtab
- WhatsNewDot indicator appears on the About tab button in the sidebar. Inside About, the What's New subtab button also shows a dot if unseen content exists.
- WhatsNewContent marks content as seen when the What's New subtab is viewed (same behavior as current tab)

### Step 4: Add section labels to TimerSettingsContent.tsx
- Insert 5 section labels: PRESETS (before timer presets + default timer), BREAKS (before break duration + auto-start break), REPETITION (before repetition mode + default reps + perpetual), BEHAVIOR (before minimize on start), ALERTS (before sound + system notification)

### Step 5: Update UnifiedSettingsModal.tsx
- Change Tab type to: 'general' | 'projects' | 'appearance' | 'labels' | 'timer' | 'about'
- Update tabs array to 6 entries: General, Projects, Appearance, Labels, Timer, About
- Update content area routing to render new components
- Change handleTabChange/handleClose theme checks: 'themes' → 'appearance'
- Move WhatsNewDot to 'about' tab button
- Update index.ts barrel exports

### Step 6: Update help text references
- In HelpSettingsContent.tsx, update any references like 'Settings → MCP' to 'Settings → About → Integrations', 'Settings → Themes' to 'Settings → Appearance', etc.

## Key Risk: Theme Dirty-State Coupling
ThemeSettingsContent uses forwardRef + useImperativeHandle for apply()/revert(). AppearanceSettingsContent must forward this ref. handleTabChange/handleClose change 'themes' → 'appearance'. Subtab switches within Appearance also check dirty state before allowing the switch.

## Verified Safe
- setSettingsInitialTab() is only ever called with undefined — no deep-link breakage
- No tests reference UI tab names — no test breakage
- All setting keys, store methods, and IPC handlers are unchanged

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Settings modal shows exactly 7 tabs in sidebar: General, Projects, Appearance, Labels, Timer, About, plus Logout at bottom
Appearance tab has a subtab bar with Theme and Priority Display; default is Theme
About tab has a subtab bar with Updates, What's New, Integrations, Help; default is Updates
WhatsNewDot indicator appears on the About tab button when unseen content exists
General tab has 6 section labels: Task Behavior, Quick Add, My Day, Notifications, System, Keyboard Shortcuts
All Notification settings (enable toggle, lead time dropdown, info box) appear in General under Notifications section with same conditional behavior
Timer tab has 5 section labels: Presets, Breaks, Repetition, Behavior, Alerts
Switching from Theme subtab to Priority Display subtab with unsaved theme changes triggers the existing unsaved-changes warning toast
Every setting from the original 11 tabs is still present and functional in the new layout
No setting keys, store methods, or IPC handlers are changed
UnifiedSettingsModal.tsx is under 150 lines after extraction
All new content components are under 150 lines each
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run test passes with all existing and new tests

stories.title: MCP Activity Logging
stories.description:
Add activity log entries for all mutating MCP tool actions so changes made by AI assistants appear in the task detail activity timeline.

## Context
The MCP server (src/main/mcp-server.ts) has 38 tools, but none write activity logs. UI-driven changes log via `logTaskActivity()` in taskStore.ts → `window.api.activityLog.create()`. MCP actions bypass this entirely, so AI-made changes are invisible in the activity timeline.

## Implementation
After each mutating MCP tool handler, call `repos.activityLog.create()` with:
- `id`: crypto.randomUUID()
- `task_id`: the affected task's ID
- `user_id`: `getUser().id` (mcp-server.ts:99)
- `action`: same action names as taskStore.ts (e.g., `status_changed`, `priority_changed`, `created`, `archived`, `label_added`, etc.)
- `old_value` / `new_value`: populated where applicable (e.g., old status name → new status name)

## MCP Tools That Need Logging (22 of 38)

**Task CRUD:**
- `create_task` → action: `created`
- `delete_task` → action: `deleted`
- `create_subtask` → action: `created` (on the subtask)

**Task Status:**
- `complete_task` → action: `status_changed` (old status name → done status name)
- `reopen_task` → action: `status_changed` (done status name → default status name)

**Field Setters:**
- `update_task` → log per changed field: `title_changed`, `status_changed`, `priority_changed`, `due_date_changed` as applicable
- `set_task_priority` → action: `priority_changed` (old level → new level)
- `set_task_due_date` → action: `due_date_changed` (old date → new date)
- `set_task_description` → action: `description_changed`
- `set_task_recurrence` → action: `recurrence_changed`

**Organization:**
- `add_task_to_my_day` → action: `pinned_to_my_day`
- `remove_task_from_my_day` → action: `unpinned_from_my_day`
- `snooze_task` → action: `due_date_changed` (snoozed to new date)
- `archive_task` → action: `archived`
- `unarchive_task` → action: `unarchived`

**Labels:**
- `assign_label_to_task` → action: `label_added` (new_value: label name)
- `remove_label_from_task` → action: `label_removed` (old_value: label name)

**Other:**
- `duplicate_task` → action: `created` (on the new task)
- `save_task_as_template` → no task-level log needed (template is a separate entity)
- `use_task_template` → action: `created` (on the new task)

**Read-only tools (no logging):** `list_tasks`, `get_task`, `list_projects`, `get_project`, `list_labels`, `list_statuses`, `search_tasks`, `list_my_day`, `list_subtasks`, `list_templates`, `set_whats_new`

## Key Files
- `src/main/mcp-server.ts` — add `repos.activityLog.create()` calls after each mutating handler
- `src/main/repositories/ActivityLogRepository.ts` — existing, no changes needed
- `src/shared/types.ts` — existing ActivityLogEntry/CreateActivityLogInput types, no changes needed

## Pattern
For each handler, fetch the task state BEFORE the mutation (for old_value), perform the mutation, then log. Example for `set_task_priority`:
```
const task = repos.tasks.findById(taskId)
const oldPriority = task.priority
repos.tasks.update(taskId, { priority: newPriority })
repos.activityLog.create({ id: crypto.randomUUID(), task_id: taskId, user_id: getUser().id, action: 'priority_changed', old_value: String(oldPriority), new_value: String(newPriority) })
```

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: All 22 mutating MCP tools write activity log entries after successful operations

Action strings match existing UI action names (created, status_changed, priority_changed, due_date_changed, archived, unarchived, label_added, label_removed, pinned_to_my_day, unpinned_from_my_day, recurrence_changed, title_changed, deleted)
old_value and new_value are populated where applicable (e.g., status change shows old and new status name)
Activity logs from MCP actions appear in the task detail panel activity timeline
getUser().id is used as the user_id for all MCP activity log entries
Read-only MCP tools do not generate activity logs
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run test passes with all existing and new tests

stories.title: MCP Task Reorder Tool
stories.description:
Add a `reorder_tasks` tool to the MCP server that allows AI assistants to reorder tasks within a project or status group.

## Context
`TaskRepository.reorder(taskIds: string[])` already exists (TaskRepository.ts:148) — it takes an ordered array of task IDs and sets `order_index` sequentially. The MCP server just needs to expose this.

## Implementation
Register a new `reorder_tasks` tool in `src/main/mcp-server.ts` with:
- **Parameters:**
  - `task_ids` (required, string array): Ordered array of task IDs in the desired display order
- **Validation:**
  - All task IDs must exist in the database (fetch each, fail if any missing)
  - Array must not be empty
- **Execution:**
  - Call `repos.tasks.reorder(taskIds)`
- **Auto-sort warning:**
  - After reorder, check the `priority_auto_sort` setting via `repos.settings.get(getUser().id, 'priority_auto_sort')`
  - If `priority_auto_sort === 'true'`, include a warning in the response: "Note: priority auto-sort is enabled in the user's settings — the visual order in the app may differ from the requested order"
- **Response:**
  - Return success with count of tasks reordered and optional auto-sort warning
- **Activity logging:**
  - Since this is a bulk operation on order_index (not a per-task semantic change), no activity log entries are needed

## Key Files
- `src/main/mcp-server.ts` — add tool definition and handler
- `src/main/repositories/TaskRepository.ts` — existing `reorder()` method at line 148, no changes needed
- `src/main/repositories/SettingsRepository.ts` — existing `get()` method to check auto-sort setting

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: New reorder_tasks MCP tool accepts an array of task IDs in the desired order
Calls TaskRepository.reorder() to set order_index values
Validates that all task IDs exist before reordering
Checks the priority_auto_sort setting; if enabled, includes a warning in the response that visual order may differ
Returns success confirmation with the number of tasks reordered
Tool is registered in the MCP server tool list with description and parameter schema
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run test passes with all existing and new tests

stories.title: Command Palette Clears Filters on Navigation
stories.description:
When navigating to a task via Cmd+K (command palette), if label filters are active that would hide the target task, the task is selected but invisible. Fix: clear label/assignee filters when the command palette navigates to a task.

## Root Cause
`CommandPalette.handleSelect()` (src/renderer/src/features/command-palette/CommandPalette.tsx:123-142) calls `viewStore.setSelectedProject()` which does NOT call `clearLabelFilters()`. Sidebar navigation paths (handleViewClick, handleProjectClick) all clear filters, but this code path doesn't.

## The Bug Pathway
1. User has label filters active in a project view
2. User opens Cmd+K and selects a task that doesn't match those filters
3. CommandPalette calls setSelectedProject(task.project_id) → switches to project view
4. Label filters remain active from step 1
5. TaskListView.filteredTasks excludes the target task (filterMode 'hide') or blurs it
6. Task is selected in store but not visible — scrollIntoView fails because no DOM element exists

## Fix
In `CommandPalette.tsx` `handleSelect` callback (~line 123), call `useLabelStore.getState().clearLabelFilters()` before calling `setSelectedProject()`. This matches the pattern used by:
- Sidebar.handleViewClick() (Sidebar.tsx:161)
- Sidebar.handleProjectClick() (Sidebar.tsx:169)

## Key Files
- `src/renderer/src/features/command-palette/CommandPalette.tsx` — handleSelect callback, add clearLabelFilters() call
- `src/renderer/src/shared/stores/labelStore.ts` — existing clearLabelFilters() method, no changes needed

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Navigating to a task via Cmd+K clears any active label filters
Navigating to a task via Cmd+K clears any active assignee filters
The target task is visible and scrolled into view after navigation
Existing sidebar navigation filter-clearing behavior is unchanged
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run test passes with all existing and new tests

stories.title: Expanded Filter System
stories.description:
Expand the filter bar from labels-only to support priority, due date range, status, assignee, and keyword filters. This is a prerequisite for Saved Views (story #47).

## Current State
The filter system (labelStore.ts) only supports label filters and assignee filters. TaskRepository.search() supports single label_id, single status_id, single priority. No UI for priority/status/due date/keyword filtering in the task list views.

## Filter Bar Design
- Labels and project scope are always visible as primary filters
- A '+ Filter' button reveals a dropdown with additional filter options: Priority, Due Date, Status, Assignee, Keyword
- Selecting an additional filter adds it as a removable chip to the filter bar
- Chips show the filter type and value, with an X button to remove
- All filters combine with AND logic
- This follows the Linear/Notion pattern

## Filter Types
1. **Labels** (existing, always visible): multi-select from project labels, OR logic between labels
2. **Project scope** (always visible): dropdown to scope to specific project or 'All Projects'
3. **Priority** (via + Filter): multi-select checkboxes (None, Low, Normal, High, Urgent)
4. **Due Date** (via + Filter): presets (Today, This Week, Overdue, No Date) + custom date range picker
5. **Status** (via + Filter): multi-select from current project's statuses
6. **Assignee** (existing, via + Filter): multi-select from project members
7. **Keyword** (via + Filter): text input matching task title and description

## Backend Changes
- Enhance `TaskSearchFilters` interface in TaskRepository.ts to support arrays:
  - `label_ids?: string[]` (multiple labels, OR logic)
  - `status_ids?: string[]` (multiple statuses)
  - `priorities?: number[]` (multiple priority levels)
  - `assigned_to_ids?: string[]` (multiple assignees)
  - `project_ids?: string[]` (multiple projects for cross-project queries)
- Update `TaskRepository.search()` SQL builder to handle IN clauses for array filters
- Add IPC handlers for the enhanced search

## Frontend Changes
- Expand filter state in labelStore (or create a new filterStore) to hold all filter types
- Create `FilterBar` component with primary filters + '+ Filter' dropdown + chips
- Create filter-specific picker components (PriorityFilterPicker, DateRangeFilterPicker, StatusFilterPicker)
- Update TaskListView, MyDayView, CalendarView to use expanded filters
- 'Clear all' button resets every filter type

## Key Files
- `src/main/repositories/TaskRepository.ts` — enhance search() and TaskSearchFilters
- `src/renderer/src/shared/stores/labelStore.ts` — expand filter state or create filterStore
- `src/renderer/src/shared/components/LabelFilterBar.tsx` — expand to full FilterBar
- `src/renderer/src/features/tasks/TaskListView.tsx` — use expanded filters

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Filter bar shows label filter and project scope by default
+ Filter button opens dropdown with Priority, Due Date, Status, Assignee, Keyword options
Selecting an additional filter adds it as a removable chip to the filter bar
Priority filter supports multi-select (None, Low, Normal, High, Urgent)
Due date filter has presets (Today, This Week, Overdue, No Date) plus custom range
Status filter supports multi-select from current project's statuses
Keyword filter matches task title and description
All filters combine with AND logic
TaskRepository.search() enhanced to support multiple labels, statuses, priorities, assignees, and projects
Filters work in project views, My Day, and Calendar
Clearing filters resets all filter types
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run test passes with all existing and new tests

stories.title: Saved Views / Smart Lists
stories.description:
Persist filter combinations as named views in the sidebar. Depends on story #46 (Expanded Filter System).

## Overview
When filters are active, a 'Save as View' button appears in the filter bar. Clicking it prompts for a name and saves the current filter state. Saved views appear in a collapsible sidebar section between Calendar and Projects. Clicking a saved view navigates to it and populates the filter bar with its stored filters (editable, not read-only).

## Database
New `saved_views` table (migration 14):
```sql
CREATE TABLE saved_views (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  project_id TEXT,  -- NULL for personal, set for shared project views
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  color TEXT DEFAULT '#6366f1',
  icon TEXT DEFAULT 'filter',
  sidebar_order INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  filter_config TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON serialized filter state
  created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
  updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);
```

## filter_config JSON Schema
```json
{
  "labelIds": ["uuid1", "uuid2"],
  "assigneeIds": ["uuid1"],
  "projectIds": ["uuid1"],
  "statusIds": ["uuid1", "uuid2"],
  "priorities": [3, 4],
  "dueDateRange": { "preset": "this_week" } | { "after": "2026-04-01", "before": "2026-04-07" },
  "keyword": "search term",
  "filterMode": "hide"
}
```

## Sidebar Section
- New collapsible 'Saved Views' section between Calendar and Projects
- Each view shows: icon + name + count badge (live count of matching tasks)
- 'Add' button to create empty view
- Drag to reorder
- Right-click context menu: Rename, Duplicate, Delete

## Save as View Flow
1. User sets filters in any view
2. 'Save as View' button appears in filter bar
3. Click → name prompt (inline input or small modal)
4. Saved → appears in sidebar immediately

## Navigation
- Click saved view → sets currentView to 'saved-view', populates filter bar with stored filters
- User can tweak filters in the bar (changes are NOT auto-saved back to the view)
- To update a saved view's filters: explicit 'Update View' button appears when filters differ from stored config

## Broken Filter Handling
- If a label/project/status referenced in filter_config no longer exists:
  - Show a warning icon on the saved view in sidebar
  - Hover tooltip: 'Missing filter: [label name] was deleted'
  - Silently drop the invalid criterion from query results
  - When user opens the view, broken chip shows in filter bar with warning styling

## Sharing
- Saved views with a project_id are visible to all members of that shared project
- Personal views (project_id NULL) are user-only

## MCP Tools
- `create_saved_view` — name, filter_config JSON
- `list_saved_views` — returns all user's views
- `delete_saved_view` — by ID

## Key Files
- `src/main/database/migrations.ts` — migration 14 for saved_views table
- `src/main/repositories/SavedViewRepository.ts` — new CRUD repository
- `src/renderer/src/shared/stores/savedViewStore.ts` — new Zustand store
- `src/renderer/src/features/sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` — add Saved Views section
- `src/renderer/src/features/views/SavedViewListView.tsx` — new view component
- `src/main/mcp-server.ts` — add MCP tools

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: New saved_views table created via migration with filter_config JSON column
Save as View button appears in filter bar when any filters are active
Saved views appear in collapsible sidebar section between Calendar and Projects with count badges
Clicking a saved view populates the filter bar with stored filters (editable)
Update View button appears when active filters differ from stored config
Saved views pull tasks cross-project or scoped per config
Saved views can be renamed, reordered via drag, and deleted
Deleted labels/projects in a view show broken filter warning with hover tooltip
Saved views with a project_id are shared with project members
MCP tools: create_saved_view, list_saved_views, delete_saved_view
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run test passes with all existing and new tests

stories.title: Productivity Stats Dashboard
stories.description:
New sidebar view (⌘3) showing completion trends, focus time, streaks with gamification, and team stats for shared projects. Uses Recharts for polished visualizations.

## Sidebar Placement
- Add 'Stats' to TOP_VIEW_ITEMS after Calendar with ⌘3 shortcut
- Shift Archive to ⌘5, Templates to ⌘6
- Icon: BarChart3 or TrendingUp from lucide-react

## Data Sources (all existing, no new tables)
- `tasks.completed_date` — task completion timestamps
- `activity_log` where action matches 'Completed X min focus session' — focus time
- `activity_log` with various action types — general activity

## Dashboard Sections

### 1. Overview Cards (top row)
- Tasks completed: today / this week / this month (counts)
- Focus time: today / this week (minutes/hours)
- Current streak (consecutive days with ≥1 completion)

### 2. Completion Chart
- Recharts BarChart showing daily task completions
- Time range selector: 7 / 30 / 90 days
- Bars colored with accent color

### 3. Focus Time Chart
- Recharts BarChart showing daily focus minutes
- Same time range selector

### 4. Streaks & Gamification
- Current streak with fire icon and animated glow when active
- Best streak badge
- Motivational message: 'You're on a X-day streak! Keep it up!' or 'Start a new streak today!'
- Calendar heatmap (GitHub-style contribution grid) showing activity density for the last 90 days
  - 7 rows (days of week) × 13 columns (weeks)
  - Color intensity based on completion count (0=empty, 1-2=light, 3-4=medium, 5+=dark)

### 5. Team Stats (for shared projects)
- Per-member completion counts and focus time
- Leaderboard-style ranking (most completions this period)
- Only visible when a shared project is selected in the project filter

## Filters
- Project dropdown: 'All Projects' or specific project
- Time range: 7 / 30 / 90 days

## Backend
- New methods in TaskRepository:
  - `getCompletionStats(userId, projectId?, dateRange)` — daily completion counts
  - `getStreakStats(userId)` — current and best streak
- New methods in ActivityLogRepository:
  - `getFocusStats(userId, projectId?, dateRange)` — daily focus minutes
  - `getTeamStats(projectId, dateRange)` — per-member completions and focus time
- IPC handlers for each

## Dependencies
- Install `recharts` package

## Key Files
- `src/main/repositories/TaskRepository.ts` — add stats query methods
- `src/main/repositories/ActivityLogRepository.ts` — add focus/team stats methods
- `src/renderer/src/features/views/StatsView.tsx` — main dashboard component
- `src/renderer/src/features/views/StatsHeatmap.tsx` — calendar heatmap component
- `src/renderer/src/features/sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` — add Stats nav item
- `src/renderer/src/shared/stores/viewStore.ts` — add 'stats' to ViewId union

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Stats view accessible from sidebar with ⌘3 shortcut
Overview cards show tasks completed today/week/month and focus time today/week
Recharts bar chart shows daily completions for selected time range
Recharts bar chart shows daily focus minutes for selected time range
Current streak displayed with fire icon and gamification messaging
Best streak displayed as a badge
Calendar heatmap shows 90-day activity density (GitHub contribution style)
Time range selector with 7/30/90 day options
Project filter dropdown (all projects or specific)
Team stats show per-member completions and focus time for shared projects
All stats computed from existing data (completed_date and activity_log)
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run test passes with all existing and new tests

stories.title: Project Areas / Folders
stories.description:
Group projects into collapsible single-level areas in the sidebar. Each user sees their own area assignments — not synced to other members of shared projects.

## Database
New migration 15:
```sql
CREATE TABLE project_areas (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  color TEXT DEFAULT '#888888',
  icon TEXT DEFAULT 'folder',
  sidebar_order INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  is_collapsed INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
  updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);

ALTER TABLE projects ADD COLUMN area_id TEXT REFERENCES project_areas(id) ON DELETE SET NULL;
```

## Sidebar Rendering
- Projects grouped by area in sidebar
- Ungrouped projects (area_id = NULL) appear above area groups
- Each area header shows: color dot + area name + project count + collapse toggle
- Collapsed areas hide their projects but still show the header
- Collapse state persists (is_collapsed column)

## Area Management
- 'New Area' option in sidebar (near project Add button) or in project settings
- Area context menu (right-click): Rename, Change Color, Delete
- Deleting an area sets area_id = NULL on its projects (ungroups, doesn't delete)

## Project Assignment
- In project settings: dropdown to assign project to an area
- Drag-drop: drag a project onto an area header to assign it
- Drag a project out of an area (to ungrouped zone) to unassign

## Ordering
- Areas are reorderable via drag (updates sidebar_order)
- Projects within an area are ordered by their existing sidebar_order
- Dragging a project between areas updates area_id

## Per-User Scoping
- area_id on projects is a local-only field (not synced via Supabase)
- Each user's area assignments are independent
- The project_areas table is user-scoped (user_id column)

## MCP Tools
- `create_area` — name, color
- `list_areas` — returns all user's areas with project counts
- `update_area` — rename, recolor, reorder
- `delete_area` — ungroups projects, deletes area
- `assign_project_to_area` — project_id, area_id (or null to ungroup)

## Key Files
- `src/main/database/migrations.ts` — migration 15
- `src/main/repositories/ProjectAreaRepository.ts` — new CRUD repository
- `src/renderer/src/shared/stores/projectAreaStore.ts` — new Zustand store
- `src/renderer/src/features/sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` — grouped rendering logic
- `src/renderer/src/features/projects/ProjectSettingsModal.tsx` — area assignment dropdown
- `src/shared/types.ts` — add ProjectArea interface, extend Project with area_id
- `src/main/mcp-server.ts` — add MCP tools

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: New project_areas table and area_id column on projects via migration
Users can create, rename, reorder, and delete areas
Projects grouped by area in sidebar with ungrouped projects above areas
Areas collapsible with persisted collapse state
Projects draggable between areas and to ungrouped zone
One project belongs to max one area
Deleting an area ungroups its projects without deleting them
Area assignment is per-user (not synced in shared projects)
Assign project to area via project settings dropdown or drag-drop
MCP tools: create_area, list_areas, update_area, delete_area, assign_project_to_area
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run test passes with all existing and new tests
